title: Working with Data Frames and Tibbles

intro.title: Creating Data Frames
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Creating Data Frames is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.

challenges.title: Easy: Use data.frame
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write R code that uses `data.frame` related to **Creating Data Frames**.

challenges.title: Easy: Print a number
challenges.promptMarkdown: Print the number `100` using `print()`.

challenges.title: Easy: Assign a variable
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create variable `x` with `<-` and set it to `5`, then print `x`.

challenges.title: Medium: dplyr pipe
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `%>%` with `filter()` on `mtcars` where `mpg > 20`.

challenges.title: Medium: Two-step snippet
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `data.frame` and `nrow` in one script.

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **Creating Data Frames** in a short script using `data.frame`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **Creating Data Frames** (include `data.frame`).

questions.prompt: What best describes Creating Data Frames?

choices.label: A core R concept for data analysis

questions.prompt: Which function prints output in R?
R is widely used for:
What assignment operator is preferred in R?
Missing values in R are represented by:
Which function combines values into a vector?
Data frames are:
Type the function to get help on a topic.
Expert: Vectorized operations in R:
Expert: Name one keyword from Creating Data Frames.

intro.title: Joins Basics
intro.bodyMarkdown:
Joins Basics is a core topic when learning R for data analysis, statistics, and reproducible research.

Practicing this topic builds fluency with R syntax, data structures, and the tidyverse ecosystem.

Run the sample code below, experiment in the editor, then work through the challenges.
intro.sandboxCode:
library(dplyr)
left_join(data.frame(id = 1:2, a = c("x", "y")),
              data.frame(id = 1:2, b = c(10, 20)), by = "id")

challenges.title: Medium: Vector basics
challenges.promptMarkdown: Create `v <- c(1, 2, 3)` and print `sum(v)`.

challenges.title: Hard: Conditional output
challenges.promptMarkdown: Set `n <- 10`. If `n > 0`, print `"positive"`.

challenges.title: Hard: Function call
challenges.promptMarkdown: Use `length()` on `c(4, 8, 15)` and print the result.

challenges.title: Expert: Topic practice
challenges.promptMarkdown: Combine ideas from **slice and sample** in a short script using `slice`.

challenges.title: Expert: Multi-line script
challenges.promptMarkdown: Write at least two lines of R related to **slice and sample** (include `slice`).
